title: FAI Context Schema
description: Lightweight context reference for standalone LEGO blocks. Makes an individual agent, skill, or instruction aware of the broader FAI ecosystem — which knowledge it assumes, which WAF pillars it respects, which plays it's compatible with, and what quality gates it should pass. This is the standalone-to-wired bridge.

assumes.description: FROOT knowledge module IDs this primitive assumes are available (e.g., 'R2-RAG-Architecture', 'T3-Production-Patterns'). When used standalone, these are informational. When used inside a play, the FAI Engine resolves them.

waf.description: WAF pillars this primitive respects or enforces.

compatiblePlays.description: Solution play IDs this primitive is tested and verified to work with. The FAI Engine uses this for auto-wiring.

groundedness.description: Expected groundedness threshold when this primitive runs.

costPerQuery.description: Expected cost per invocation in USD.

evaluation.description: Quality expectations when this primitive runs. Used by the FAI Engine to set evaluation thresholds.

requires.description: Other primitives this one depends on (e.g., a skill that requires a specific agent or hook). Expressed as relative paths.
